diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index 0734010..a354e3f 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -463,2 +463,26 @@
   - No new R&D.
 - Watch next week: whether older enterprise offers finally produce acceptances/counters once we stop refreshing them, enterprise lead volume with ads off, and whether higher support reduces S2 issue age/churn.
+
+## Week 27 / Day 182
+
+- Cash: $6,004,488. Individual subscribers fell to 133,392 after 5,908 cancellations; new subscribers only 1,177. Still zero enterprise subscribed seats.
+- Cancellations this week were almost entirely S2 A: 5,818 cancellations, avg effective price $104.22, start days 26-152. This points to support/retention pressure on the mature S2 book rather than low product quality.
+- S2 issues: 10,622 open, avg age 8.03 days, max 125. Issue age remains a major churn risk.
+- Enterprise pipeline before response had 630 fresh system-waiting leads and a very large agent-waiting backlog. I again answered only the fresh system-waiting leads to avoid resetting older negotiations.
+- Responded to 630 fresh enterprise leads:
+  - D_E02 333: A $59, B $69, C $99.
+  - D_E08 103: A $119, B $139, C $169.
+  - D_E09 82: A $79, B $99, C $129.
+  - E1 10: A $19, B $29, C $49.
+  - E2 28: A $79, B $89, C $119.
+  - E3 74: A $89, B $99, C $119.
+- Latest open enterprise check after response: all 3,222 open threads have latest sender=agent.
+- Week 26 cash flow: subscription payments $3,026,184; ops -$1,169,000; compute -$984,660; capacity -$196,000; dev -$116,900; ads $0.
+- Changes:
+  - Added S2 ongoing promotion of $10 to reduce renewal friction.
+  - Raised ops to $234k/day total: global $50k, S2 $150k, S3 $5k, enterprise targets $29k.
+  - Ads remain off.
+  - Dev unchanged at $16.7k/day total.
+  - Capacity remains tier 6.
+  - No new R&D.
+- Watch next week: whether S2 promotion/support reduces churn, whether S2 issue age improves, and whether enterprise threads finally produce acceptances/counters.
